Course Title: AI in Medical Imaging: False Confidence Risks
Category: Healthcare & Life Sciences: Medical Services, Pharmacy, Biotechnology, Hospitals
Who is this for: Healthcare professionals, radiologists, medical technologists, clinical staff, physicians, nurses, medical administrators, biotech professionals, hospital staff
Team or Dept this is for: Healthcare Operations, Medical Services, Radiology Department, Quality Assurance, Risk Management, Clinical Operations, Patient Safety
Description: Missed or incorrect findings in radiology and diagnostics.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with healthcare or clinical settings is helpful, though no prior knowledge of artificial intelligence or medical imaging technology is required to follow this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key ways in which AI tools in medical imaging can produce false confidence, and explain how missed or incorrect findings arise in radiology and diagnostics. You will also apply practical strategies to critically evaluate AI-generated results, helping to reduce diagnostic errors and support safer patient outcomes.
